fr.elements: Main Building; Detached Structure; Front Driveway; Landscaped Area; Open Green Space
fr.recommendations: Enhancing emergency responder access to the property could be supported by maintaining a clear and unobstructed front driveway, especially considering the single access point and the presence of multiple structures. Ensuring that the driveway is well-marked and sufficiently wide could facilitate the movement of emergency vehicles and personnel. Clearly delineating the boundaries of the landscaped area and open green space could assist responders in efficiently navigating the site. Marking entrances and exits to each structure with visible signage may enhance visibility and ease of access, while designated staging areas on the property could support efficient deployment of emergency operations. Regular maintenance of the surrounding landscape to manage overgrowth could further ensure that responders can move freely and safely across the property, optimizing their response efforts.

wildfire.conditions: Structure Surrounded by Vegetation; Scattered Trees; Vegetated Perimeter; Open Terrain; Vegetation Adjacent to Structure; Limited Defensible Space; Vegetation Adjacent to Fence; Long Driveway; Scattered Shrubs; Open Fuel Break
wildfire.recommendations: Enhancing defensible space could be achieved by managing vegetation adjacent to the structure and along the fence to limit the potential for fire spread. Increasing separation between scattered trees and shrubs around the property may reduce fuel continuity and improve wildfire resilience. Maintaining open terrain and preserving the existing fuel break could support long-term vegetation management and act as a barrier to fire progression. Given the long driveway, ensuring it is clear of overgrown vegetation could enhance firefighter access and support suppression efforts. Strategic vegetation reduction around the structure and towards the vegetated perimeter might improve defensible space and reduce potential structure exposure.
